required_header.download_date: ClinicalTrials.gov processed this data on February 2026
required_header.link_text: Link to the current ClinicalTrials.gov record.

clinical_study.brief_title: Ipilimumab Plus Nivolumab in Metastatic Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma with IMDC Intermediate Risk and VHL/PBRM1 Mutations

brief_summary.textblock: This study evaluates ipilimumab 1 mg/kg plus nivolumab 3 mg/kg every 3 weeks for 4 induction cycles followed by nivolumab 480 mg every 4 weeks maintenance, in patients with previously untreated metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) classified as IMDC intermediate risk. Patients with VHL and PBRM1 co-mutation are targeted as a molecular subgroup. Patients with renal vein or possible IVC involvement and concurrent adrenal or pulmonary metastases are eligible. Cytoreductive nephrectomy is deferred pending systemic response. The primary endpoint is objective response rate by RECIST 1.1 at 12 weeks.

detailed_description.textblock: Patients with histologically confirmed clear cell RCC, Stage IV (M1), classified as IMDC intermediate risk (1-2 risk factors) are eligible. Tumor molecular profiling must confirm VHL pathogenic mutation and PBRM1 pathogenic mutation. BAP1 loss is permitted. Patients with anemia (hemoglobin below lower limit of normal) and hypercalcemia (corrected calcium above upper limit of normal) as the two IMDC risk factors are the target population. Patients with primary tumor in situ (nephrectomy deferred) are eligible. Adrenal and pulmonary metastases are permitted. Hypothyroidism on stable thyroid hormone replacement and hypertension on antihypertensive therapy do not constitute exclusion criteria.

clinical_study.study_design: Intervention Model: Single Group Assignment, Primary Purpose: Treatment

intervention.intervention_name: Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg + Nivolumab 3 mg/kg IV q3w (induction) followed by Nivolumab 480 mg q4w (maintenance)

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Histologically confirmed clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC), ISUP grade 2 or 3
- Stage IV metastatic disease (M1)
- IMDC intermediate risk: 1 or 2 of the following risk factors present: anemia (hemoglobin below lower limit of normal), hypercalcemia (corrected calcium above upper limit of normal), time from diagnosis to treatment less than 1 year, KPS less than 80, neutrophilia, thrombocytosis
- VHL pathogenic mutation confirmed by tumor NGS
- PBRM1 pathogenic mutation confirmed by tumor NGS
- No prior systemic therapy for metastatic RCC (treatment-naive)
- Primary tumor in situ or prior nephrectomy both acceptable
- Adrenal, pulmonary, or lymph node metastases permitted
- Age 18 years or older
- ECOG performance status 0 or 1
- Adequate organ function: ANC greater than or equal to 1.5, platelets greater than or equal to 100, creatinine less than or equal to 2.0x ULN, AST/ALT less than or equal to 3x ULN, total bilirubin less than or equal to 1.5x ULN
- Hypothyroidism on stable levothyroxine replacement is permitted
- Hypertension managed with antihypertensive agents is permitted
Exclusion Criteria:
- Non-clear cell RCC histology (papillary, chromophobe, collecting duct, or sarcomatoid without clear cell component)
- IMDC favorable risk (0 risk factors) or poor risk (3 or more risk factors)
- MET amplification present
- Active brain metastases (untreated or symptomatic)
- Prior anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L1, or anti-CTLA-4 immunotherapy
- Prior systemic therapy for RCC (VEGF-TKI, mTOR inhibitor, or other)
- Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression
- Active or chronic hepatitis B or C infection
- Uncontrolled hypercalcemia (corrected calcium greater than 12 mg/dL despite bisphosphonate or denosumab treatment)
- Active corticosteroid use (prednisone greater than 10 mg/day or equivalent) within 2 weeks prior to enrollment
- Shellfish allergy alone is not an exclusion; IV contrast allergy requires premedication protocol per institutional guidelines

facility.name: Kidney Cancer Program, Academic Medical Center
